DB2 database error - Connection or operation failed. Check DB2 logs for details.
ExitCodes.Note: This is a DB2-specific error code. Check DB2 diagnostic logs (db2diag.log) for detailed error information.

ExitCodes.Description: DB2 database error - Connection or operation failed. Check DB2 logs for details. (0x41303)
Custom application error - Check application logs for details. (0x40)

Notes.General: Exit codes can be standard Windows error codes, application-specific codes, or custom script exit codes. Codes > 2147483647 are typically HRESULT error codes (signed 32-bit integers displayed as unsigned).
Notes.HRESULT: HRESULT codes are 32-bit values where the high bit indicates success (0) or failure (1). Common format: 0x8007XXXX where 8007 indicates a Windows error.
Notes.CustomCodes: Exit codes like 40, 41303 are application-specific. Check the application's documentation or logs for specific meaning.
Notes.PowerShell: PowerShell scripts typically use exit codes 0 (success) or 1 (failure), but can use any integer.
Notes.BatchFiles: Batch files use ERRORLEVEL which is set by the last command. Common values: 0 (success), 1-255 (various errors).
Notes.Interpretation: To interpret an exit code: 1) Check if it's a standard Windows error code, 2) Check if it's application-specific, 3) Check application logs for context, 4) Convert large numbers to hex to see if it's an HRESULT code.

40.Description: Custom application error - Check DedgeCommonFilesHandling script/application logs for specific details
40.Action: Review the script or application that runs this task to understand what exit code 40 means in that context

41303.Description: DB2 database error - Database connection or operation failed
41303.Action: Check DB2 diagnostic logs (db2diag.log) and verify database connectivity, credentials, and network access
